title: Gestational Diabetes - Poorly Controlled
chief_complaint: I'm 30 weeks pregnant. My glucose tolerance test was abnormal. I've been trying to control my sugars with diet but they're still high. I'm worried about the baby.

physical_exam.Obstetric: Fundal height 34cm (large for dates - macrosomia). Fetal HR 140. Normal amniotic fluid clinically.

hidden_data.ogtt: 1h 50g GCT: 180 mg/dL (positive screen). 3h 100g OGTT: Fasting 105 (abnormal ≥95), 1h 200 (abnormal ≥180), 2h 165 (abnormal ≥155), 3h 145 (abnormal ≥140). 3 of 4 values abnormal = GDM diagnosed.
hidden_data.glucose_log: Fasting glucose 120-140 (goal <95). Postprandial 1h 160-200 (goal <140). Uncontrolled despite diet.
hidden_data.ob_us: Fetal macrosomia: EFW 2,100g (>95th percentile for 30 weeks). Abdominal circumference >99th percentile. Polyhydramnios (AFI 28cm - normal 8-24). BPP 8/8.
hidden_data.fetal_echo: Fetal echo: Normal cardiac structure. Mild septal hypertrophy (insulin effect).
hidden_data.cmp: Normal renal and hepatic function. No ketones.

correct_diagnosis: Gestational Diabetes Mellitus (Class A2 - Requiring Medication) with Fetal Macrosomia and Polyhydramnios
key_learning_points: GDM = glucose intolerance first recognized during pregnancy. Affects 5-10% of pregnancies. Pathophysiology: Placental hormones (hPL, progesterone, cortisol) → insulin resistance → hyperglycemia

Screening: Universal at 24-28 weeks. 1h 50g GCT (non-fasting): ≥130-140 = positive screen (80% sensitivity). 3h 100g OGTT (fasting): Carpenter-Coustan criteria: Fasting 95, 1h 180, 2h 155, 3h 140. 2/4 abnormal = GDM. Early screening (1st trimester) for high-risk women (obesity, prior GDM, family history, PCOS)

Fetal complications: Macrosomia (>4,000g - most common), Shoulder dystocia, Birth trauma (brachial plexus, clavicle fracture), Neonatal hypoglycemia (fetal hyperinsulinemia → rebound hypoglycemia after delivery), Hyperbilirubinemia, Polyhydramnios, Stillbirth (increased risk with poor control), Long-term: Childhood obesity and diabetes

Management: 1) Diet: Medical nutrition therapy (carbohydrate-controlled, 30-35 kcal/kg). 2) Exercise: 30 min daily. 3) Glucose monitoring: Fasting <95, 1h postprandial <140, 2h <120. 4) Medication if >2 weeks of diet fails: Insulin (first-line - does not cross placenta). NPH 0.2 U/kg at bedtime (for fasting hyperglycemia). Rapid-acting insulin (Lispro/Aspart) before meals. Metformin (second-line - crosses placenta but appears safe. Easier, less weight gain. 30-50% eventually need insulin). Glyburide: Not recommended (crosses placenta, higher rates of macrosomia and neonatal hypoglycemia)

Intrapartum: Maintain glucose 80-110 mg/dL. Insulin drip if needed. After placenta delivery: Insulin requirements drop dramatically (stop insulin, monitor glucose). Postpartum: 2h 75g OGTT at 6-12 weeks postpartum (reclassify - most revert to normal, 30-50% develop T2DM within 10 years). Lifestyle modifications, annual diabetes screening
